blocks.content: Congratulations on completing this journey! Job well done!

Throughout this journey, you have:

- Learned how label combinations multiply into unique active series and log streams, and that this is what you're billed for
- Explored your own stack's cardinality with the Cardinality tab in the Cost Management and Billing app
- Learned the rules for metric labels: bounded values only, added only when queried, and audited at ingestion setup
- Reviewed Adaptive Metrics recommendations to reduce cardinality you already have
- Applied the logs decision framework: labels for origin, structured metadata for high-cardinality fields, filter expressions for the rest
- Learned to correlate signals so high-cardinality detail lives on logs, not metric labels

Keep the two questions from this path in your team's review habits: *Is this label value bounded?* and *Will anyone query by it?* Most cardinality problems never happen when someone asks those questions at instrumentation time.

---

### More to explore (optional)

Continue your cost management journey by exploring:

- [Understand billing and usage](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/billing-usage/)
- [Optimize metrics with Adaptive Metrics](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/adaptive-metrics/)
- [Optimize logs with Adaptive Logs](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/adaptive-logs/)
